message:
test(config,state): add tests for config.Diff and state.ListExpiredPendingApprovals (bd-17guh)

config.Diff: 20 tests covering nil input, default-returns-no-diffs, and
per-section field changes (theme, agents, tmux, agent_mail, alerts,
checkpoints, resilience, context_rotation, ensemble+synthesis+cache+budget+
early_stop, CASS, health, DCG integration). Coverage 78.5% → 82.4%.

state.ListExpiredPendingApprovals: 5 tests covering empty store, expired vs
active filtering, non-pending status exclusion, multiple expired ordering,
and full field population. Coverage 84.8% → 85.6%.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-16y6i","title":"Unit Tests: ntm assign --pane direct assignment validation","description":"## Test Coverage
Unit tests for ntm assign --pane direct assignment.

## Test Cases

### Basic Assignment
- Assign bd-xyz to pane 3
- Verify assignment created
- Verify prompt sent

### Validation
- Pane must exist
- Bead must not be blocked (unless --ignore-deps)
- Bead must not be already assigned (unless --force)
- Agent should be idle (unless --force)

### Override Flags
- --force overrides busy check
- --ignore-deps allows blocked bead assignment
- Both flags together

### Error Cases
- Invalid pane number -> error
- Blocked bead without flag -> error
- Already assigned bead -> error

### File Reservations
- Create reservations on assignment
- Handle reservation conflicts

## Test File

 {"id":"bd-16yf","title":"E2E Tests: ntm assign full workflow with real agents","description":"## Test Coverage
Comprehensive end-to-end test of ntm assign with real tmux and agents, covering all assignment features.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Basic Assignment Flow
1. Create test workspace with beads
2. Spawn test agents (--cc=2)
3. Run ntm assign
4. Verify assignments distributed
5. Verify prompts received by agents
6. Verify assignment tracking state

### 2. Strategy Testing
- Test --strategy=balanced (default)
- Test --strategy=speed
- Test --strategy=quality
- Test --strategy=dependency
- Test --strategy=round-robin

### 3. Pane-Specific Assignment (--pane)
1. Spawn agents
2. Run ntm assign --pane=2 bd-xyz
3. Verify only pane 2 got assignment
4. Test --force flag for busy agents
5. Test --ignore-deps for blocked beads

### 4. Reassignment (--reassign)
1. Assign bead to agent 1
2. Run ntm assign --reassign bd-xyz --to-pane=3
3. Verify old assignment marked "reassigned"
4. Verify new assignment created
5. Verify file reservations transferred

### 5. Clear Assignment (--clear)
1. Assign beads to agents
2. Run ntm assign --clear bd-xyz
3. Verify assignment removed
4. Verify file reservations released
5. Test --clear-pane for bulk clear
6. Test --clear-failed for failed assignments

### 6. Combined spawn --assign
1. Run ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --assign
2. Verify agents spawned AND assignments created
3. Test with --strategy flag

## Detailed Logging Requirements

### Log Format Standard
```
[TIMESTAMP] [LEVEL] [COMPONENT] Message
Example: [2026-01-19T12:00:00Z] [INFO] [SETUP] Creating test workspace
```

### Log Levels
- DEBUG: Detailed command output, intermediate state
- INFO: Test step progress, milestones
- WARN: Non-fatal issues, skipped assertions
- ERROR: Test failures, assertion failures
- PASS: Successful assertions/test completion

### Required Log Points

#### Setup Phase
```
[INFO] [SETUP] Creating test workspace at /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
[DEBUG] [SETUP] Running: mkdir -p /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$/beads
[INFO] [SETUP] Creating 5 test beads with dependencies
[DEBUG] [SETUP] Bead bd-test-1: "Test feature A"
[INFO] [SETUP] Workspace ready: /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
```

#### Spawn Phase
```
[INFO] [SPAWN] Spawning 2 Claude agents
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Running: ntm spawn $SESSION --cc=2
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Output: {"session":"$SESSION","agents":[...]}
[INFO] [SPAWN] Waiting for agents to become ready...
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Pane 2 status: ready
[DEBUG] [SPAWN] Pane 3 status: ready
[INFO] [SPAWN] All 2 agents ready
```

#### Test Execution Phase
```
[INFO] [TEST:basic_assign] Starting basic assignment test
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Running: ntm assign $SESSION --auto
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Output: {"strategy":"balanced","assigned":[...]}
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Expected: 2 assignments
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Actual: 2 assignments
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Assignment count matches
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Checking pane 2 assignment...
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Expected: bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [TEST:basic_assign] Actual: bd-test-1
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Pane 2 assignment correct
[PASS] [TEST:basic_assign] Basic assignment test PASSED
```

#### Assertion Format
```
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Checking: pane 2 should have assignment bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Expected: bd-test-1
[DEBUG] [ASSERT] Actual: bd-test-1
[PASS] [ASSERT] Assertion passed
```

#### Failure Logging
```
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Assertion failed: file reservation transfer
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Expected: file_reservations_transferred=true
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Actual: file_reservations_transferred=false
[ERROR] [TEST:reassign] Context: Previous pane=2, Target pane=3
[DEBUG] [TEST:reassign] Assignment store state: {...}
[DEBUG] [TEST:reassign] Agent Mail reservation state: {...}
[FAIL] [TEST:reassign] Test failed at step 5
```

#### Cleanup Phase
```
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Killing test session $SESSION
[DEBUG] [CLEANUP] Running: ntm kill $SESSION
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Removing test workspace
[DEBUG] [CLEANUP] Running: rm -rf /tmp/ntm-e2e-$$
[INFO] [CLEANUP] Cleanup complete
```

### Log File Structure
```
/tmp/ntm-e2e-assign-full-YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.log
├── Header (test info, timestamp, version)
├── Setup section
├── Test sections (one per test suite)
│   ├── Test name
│   ├── Steps with DEBUG/INFO
│   ├── Assertions with PASS/FAIL
│   └── Test result
├── Summary section
│   ├── Total tests
│   ├── Passed
│   ├── Failed
│   └── Skipped
└── Cleanup section
```

## Test Script Structure
```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail

# Configuration
LOG_FILE=/tmp/ntm-e2e-assign-full-$(date +%Y%m%d-%H%M%S).log
SESSION="ntm-e2e-test-$$"
TEST_DIR="/tmp/ntm-e2e-$$"
VERBOSE=${VERBOSE:-0}

# Logging functions
log_debug() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [DEBUG] $*" >> "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_info()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_warn()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [WARN] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_error() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [ERROR] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" >&2; }
log_pass()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE"; }
log_fail()  { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" | tee -a "$LOG_FILE" >&2; }

assert_eq() {
    local desc="$1" expected="$2" actual="$3"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Checking: $desc"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Expected: $expected"
    log_debug "[ASSERT] Actual: $actual"
    if [[ "$expected" == "$actual" ]]; then
        log_pass "[ASSERT] $desc"
        return 0
    else
        log_fail "[ASSERT] $desc"
        log_error "[ASSERT] Expected: $expected, Got: $actual"
        return 1
    fi
}

run_cmd() {
    local cmd="$1"
    log_debug "[CMD] Running: $cmd"
    local output
    output=$(eval "$cmd" 2>&1) || {
        log_error "[CMD] Command failed: $cmd"
        log_error "[CMD] Output: $output"
        return 1
    }
    log_debug "[CMD] Output: $output"
    echo "$output"
}

# ... test functions ...

main() {
    log_info "=========================================="
    log_info "NTM Assign E2E Tests - $(date)"
    log_info "Session: $SESSION"
    log_info "=========================================="
    
    # Run tests, summarize results
}

trap cleanup EXIT
main
```

## Test File
tests/e2e/test_assign_full.sh

## Dependencies
This test depends on all ntm assign features being implemented:
- bd-k3zq (ntm assign)
- bd-1gbt5 (--reassign)
- bd-2a5yf (--clear)
- bd-2fxyp (--pane)

 {"id":"bd-17h4l","title":"Task: E2E tests for Audit Log System","description":"E2E tests for Audit Log System (bd-3pbe).

## Test Scenarios

1. Audit Log Creation E2E: Initialize NTM with audit logging, perform actions, verify log exists with entries
2. Audit Query E2E: Create entries, query all, query by event type, verify filtering works
3. Audit Export E2E: Generate entries, export to CSV, verify header and row count

5. Audit Retention E2E: Generate entries, apply retention policy, verify old entries archived

5. Export produces valid CSV with header
6. Integrity verification detects tampering
7. Retention policy archives old entries
8. Exit code 0 on all pass, non-zero on any failure

 {"id":"bd-17xu8","title":"Task: Implement early stopping based on marginal utility","description":"Stop ensemble early when new findings rate drops.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents (default: 3)
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if new findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents (default: 2)
    Enabled             bool    // Can be disabled
}

type EarlyStopEvaluator struct {
    Config   *EarlyStopConfig
    History  []AgentMetrics
}

type AgentMetrics struct {
    AgentIndex    int
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    SimilarityToOthers float64
}

func NewEarlyStopEvaluator(config *EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopEvaluator
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) RecordAgent(metrics AgentMetrics)
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) ShouldStop() (bool, string)
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) GetFindingsVelocity() float64
func (e *EarlyStopEvaluator) GetAverageSimilarity() float64
```

## Stop Conditions
- Findings velocity drops below threshold for WindowSize agents
- Output similarity exceeds threshold (redundant agents)
- Both conditions evaluated after MinAgentsBeforeStop

 {"id":"bd-32ck","title":"Feature: Extend checkpoints with assignments/progress","description":"## Overview
Checkpointing already exists. Extend it to capture **assignment/progress context** so restores are more useful for multi‑agent work.

## Scope
- Include current assignments (bead → agent) in checkpoint metadata
- Include BV snapshot summary (ready/blocked counts) at checkpoint time
- Ensure robot outputs (`--robot-checkpoint-*` if present) include these fields
- Keep existing checkpoint storage format backward‑compatible (additive fields)

## Tests
- Unit tests for new checkpoint fields (serialize/deserialize)
- E2E: create checkpoint during active assignments; restore; verify summary emitted

## Logging
`log.Printf("[E2E-CHECKPOINT] session=%s assignments=%d ready=%d blocked=%d", …)

## Acceptance Criteria
- Checkpoints remain readable even without new fields
- New fields present when assignment tracking is enabled
- Restore flow surfaces assignment summary","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-19T08:41:12.436057119Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T08:08:38.676143381Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T08:08:38.676076607Z","close_reason":"All acceptance criteria met: (1) backward compat via omitempty, (2) assignments+BV captured when enabled, (3) restore surfaces assignment/BV summary in RestoreResult and via slog.  {"id":"bd-32svm","title":"Task: Wire checkpoint export/import --redact-secrets to shared engine","description":"## Scope
Ensure checkpoint export/import uses the canonical redaction engine instead of maintaining separate regex lists.

## Context
A prior checkpoint export/import implementation introduced `--redact-secrets` with an embedded regex list. We want a single source of truth.

## Requirements
- Preserve CLI flags and behavior (`--redact-secrets`) for compatibility.
- Internally delegate to the shared redaction engine + config.
- Ensure manifest/checksum logic isn’t impacted.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No duplicate regex list remains in checkpoint export code.
- Unit tests cover at least one end-to-end export redaction case.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:22:57.777780055Z","created_by":"OrangeWolf","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:56.140520893Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:58:56.140326Z","close_reason":"Completed","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-1sjqs","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-32svm","depends_on_id":"bd-20xv1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:48:38Z","created_by":"import"}]}
